topic.what: Staging systems translating Sydney System histology into cancer risk scores (stages 0-IV) based on atrophy (OLGA) or intestinal metaplasia (OLGIM).
topic.how: Maps extent/severity of atrophy or IM across antral and corpus biopsies into a 0-IV stage, with III-IV identifying high cancer risk.
topic.why_matters: Operationalizes the Correa cascade into clinical decision-making — tells clinicians which patients need surveillance and which don't.

characteristics.value: Stages III-IV identify patients with significantly elevated cancer risk
characteristics.vs_baseline: Sydney System alone does not quantify risk

characteristics.value: OLGIM has better inter-observer agreement than OLGA
characteristics.vs_baseline: OLGIM superior to OLGA for reproducibility

novelty: First cancer risk staging system for gastritis
OLGIM variant improved reproducibility by using IM instead of atrophy
Made surveillance intervals evidence-based rather than arbitrary
open_problems: Requires invasive biopsy — no serological equivalent with same accuracy
Cut-off between stage II and III has moderate uncertainty
Not widely adopted outside specialist centers
